reasoning: The empirical base rate for ACLED "any-increase" questions over a 360-day average is approximately 22.5%, largely due to the right-skewed nature of protest event distributions (a few months with large spikes raise the annual average, meaning most 30-day periods are below average). Following the 2024 elections, Anura Kumara Dissanayake secured a large majority, leading to relative political stability compared to the 2022 crisis period, though there are still ongoing localized protests and suppression of dissent. There is no evidence of an impending massive wave of protests in late 2025/early 2026. Furthermore, the first window (ending Nov 16, 2025) is mostly in the past relative to the Nov 9 cutoff, and no major spikes were recorded recently, permitting a slightly sub-base-rate estimate for the nearest horizon. Probabilities are regressed toward 0.5 for more distant horizons to reflect increasing uncertainty.
